summary: Iteration 5 backend-only regression check for code-quality refactor (bool() truthy check, defensive default lists, parse_excel_bytes split into 3 helpers). All 9 requested checks pass. Behavior is identical to iteration 4: same counts (persons=76, details=72, lines=15), same schedule result for the standard configs (req=40, ass=38, short=2), same line_keys [X-Smart, X-Smart #2, E2, Spares], skill filter still bool-true only, no duplicate assignments across cells, specialist-first ordering preserved. Adjust set + clear work correctly. Upload-excel via refactored helpers returns persons=76 details=72. Export produces a valid xlsx with correct Content-Disposition and Content-Type. Analytics returns the expected 3-key shape. No errors in supervisor logs.
passed: GET /api/persons -> 76 records; every person.skills value is bool (truthy-check regression check passed)
GET /api/details -> 72 rows; row_name populated on all; persons_required is int on all
POST /api/schedule -> line_keys=[E2, Spares, X-Smart, X-Smart #2]; req=40 ass=38 short=2; 0 skill-mismatch assignments; 0 duplicate person across cells (matches iteration_4)
POST /api/schedule/{date}/adjust action=set -> swap person now only in target X-Smart p&c assembly (removed from Spares cell as expected)
POST /api/schedule/{date}/adjust action=clear -> target cell assigned_person_ids=[], shortage=2/required=2; cell stays unassigned after implicit regeneration
POST /api/upload-excel with /app/backend/seed_data.xlsx -> {persons:76, details:72}; stats confirm reseed OK via refactored _parse_persons_sheet / _parse_lines_sheet / _coerce_int

GET /api/export/{date}?shift=day -> 200, Content-Type application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, Content-Disposition attachment; filename=schedule-2026-01-15-day.xlsx; body is a valid xlsx zip (9 entries)
GET /api/analytics/shortage -> keys={top_short_details, top_short_lines, history}; sample history includes required/assigned/shortage per date
Supervisor backend logs: no new errors/exceptions/tracebacks during the run
